expectations.text: behavioral_check: Pushes back on editing the installed `DLCPosV1` source — names the Core Directive 'Do not edit the installed Spyglass package' (or equivalent reasoning) and gives at least two concrete reasons (in-DB schema desync, `pip install -e .` reverts edits, behavior changes affect everyone)
expectations.evidence: Section 'Why editing DLCPosV1 is a bad idea' lists 4 reasons: schema-vs-MySQL drift, drop()'s cascade impact, shared schema affects everyone, next pip install can fail to reconcile. Doesn't name the Core Directive verbatim but the equivalent reasoning is present.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does NOT recommend `dj.alter()` on `DLCPosV1` either — that mutates the shared schema for all consumers, and the Python class will desync on next install
expectations.evidence: Doesn't recommend dj.alter(); presents ALTER TABLE as one of two harmful options 'puts your local schema out of sync with everyone else'. Recommendation is downstream table, not alter.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Recommends a NEW `dj.Computed` table in the user's OWN schema module, FK'd to `DLCPosV1`, with `SpyglassMixin` first in MRO — that's the correct extension shape
expectations.evidence: Recommends `class DLCPosV1Bodyparts(dj.Computed)` in 'your own schema', but does NOT include SpyglassMixin in the inheritance — the MRO requirement is missing. The structural recommendation otherwise matches.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does NOT recommend a `dj.Manual` side table as the primary solution for bodypart names that are derivable from upstream DLC pipeline state
behavioral_check: Mentions the upstream-PR path as the only legitimate way to change the core schema (not as a routine option), without conflating it with local edits
expectations.evidence: Option (c): 'Then the right move is a PR to spyglass-neurodata/spyglass that adds a Part table — DLCPosV1.BodyPart — alongside the existing definition, with a Spyglass-style schema migration. Not a local edit.'
